g.text: WHAT THIS SHEET DOES NOT DECIDE, AND WHAT IT NEVERTHELESS CONSTRAINS

g.desc: WHAT THIS SHEET DOES NOT DECIDE, AND WHAT IT NEVERTHELESS CONSTRAINS

g.text: Baud rate, frame format, parity, the framing of the command protocol and its behaviour on a corrupt or truncated frame

g.desc: Baud rate, frame format, parity, the framing of the command protocol and its behaviour on a corrupt or truncated frame

g.text: are firmware, and belong to the protocol story. Three things the wiring does settle for it:

g.desc: are firmware, and belong to the protocol story. Three things the wiring does settle for it:

g.text: * NO HARDWARE FLOW CONTROL. RTS and CTS are not wired, so the protocol has to be safe without them - a receiver that

g.desc: * NO HARDWARE FLOW CONTROL. RTS and CTS are not wired, so the protocol has to be safe without them - a receiver that

g.text: cannot keep up drops bytes rather than back-pressuring the sender.

g.desc: cannot keep up drops bytes rather than back-pressuring the sender.

g.text: * LENGTH. These are jumper wires, not a cable plant. Keep the harness under about 300 mm; at that length 115200 baud is

g.desc: * LENGTH. These are jumper wires, not a cable plant. Keep the harness under about 300 mm; at that length 115200 baud is

g.text: uneventful. Neither end is differentially driven and neither is isolated.

g.desc: uneventful. Neither end is differentially driven and neither is isolated.

g.text: * THE LINK IS ALLOWED TO BE ABSENT. R12 and R13 make an unplugged peer read as an idle line rather than as a fault,

g.desc: * THE LINK IS ALLOWED TO BE ABSENT. R12 and R13 make an unplugged peer read as an idle line rather than as a fault,

g.text: which is what lets the wall switch keep working with the Pi unplugged - a system-level requirement settled here, in

g.desc: which is what lets the wall switch keep working with the Pi unplugged - a system-level requirement settled here, in

g.text: hardware, before any firmware exists.

g.desc: hardware, before any firmware exists.

g.text: 5 V ON THIS LINK DESTROYS A BOARD - BOTH ENDS ARE 3.3 V

g.desc: 5 V ON THIS LINK DESTROYS A BOARD - BOTH ENDS ARE 3.3 V

g.text: Arduino Due SAM3X8E absolute maximum on an input pin: -0.3 V to +4.0 V

g.desc: Arduino Due SAM3X8E absolute maximum on an input pin: -0.3 V to +4.0 V

g.text: (SAM3X datasheet, table 45-1)

g.desc: (SAM3X datasheet, table 45-1)

g.text: Raspberry Pi BCM2837 not 5 V tolerant; the documented failure mode is

g.desc: Raspberry Pi BCM2837 not 5 V tolerant; the documented failure mode is

g.text: latch-up, which shorts the 3V3 rail inside the die

g.desc: latch-up, which shorts the 3V3 rail inside the die

g.text: 5 V is not "slightly over spec" on either part. It is past the absolute maximum

g.desc: 5 V is not "slightly over spec" on either part. It is past the absolute maximum

g.text: rating of one and a documented kill mechanism on the other.

g.desc: rating of one and a documented kill mechanism on the other.

g.text: THE MISTAKE THAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS is not miswiring these two boards to each other.

g.desc: THE MISTAKE THAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS is not miswiring these two boards to each other.

g.text: They are both 3.3 V, so that is safe by construction. It is reaching for a THIRD

g.desc: They are both 3.3 V, so that is safe by construction. It is reaching for a THIRD

g.text: device to watch the link: a 5 V USB-to-serial adapter, an Arduino Uno, a logic

g.desc: device to watch the link: a 5 V USB-to-serial adapter, an Arduino Uno, a logic

g.text: analyser with 5 V outputs, or a "5 V / 3.3 V" FTDI cable with the jumper on the

g.desc: analyser with 5 V outputs, or a "5 V / 3.3 V" FTDI cable with the jumper on the

g.text: wrong post. Any of those turns a debugging session into a dead board.

g.desc: wrong post. Any of those turns a debugging session into a dead board.

g.text: If you need to watch this link, use a 3.3 V adapter, and check it with a meter

g.desc: If you need to watch this link, use a 3.3 V adapter, and check it with a meter

g.text: before it touches either header: measure its idle TX against its own ground. It

g.desc: before it touches either header: measure its idle TX against its own ground. It

g.text: should read 3.3 V, not 5 V.

g.desc: should read 3.3 V, not 5 V.

g.text: R8 to R11 below reduce the damage. They do not license the mistake.

g.desc: R8 to R11 below reduce the damage. They do not license the mistake.

g.text: TX TO RX, CROSSED - AND HOW TO CHECK IT BEFORE POWERING UP

g.desc: TX TO RX, CROSSED - AND HOW TO CHECK IT BEFORE POWERING UP

g.text: Arduino Due Raspberry Pi

g.desc: Arduino Due Raspberry Pi

g.text: D18 / TX1 / PA11 26-pin pos 23 -> GPIO15 / RXD0 J8 pin 10

g.desc: D18 / TX1 / PA11 26-pin pos 23 -> GPIO15 / RXD0 J8 pin 10

g.text: D19 / RX1 / PA10 26-pin pos 24 <- GPIO14 / TXD0 J8 pin 8

g.desc: D19 / RX1 / PA10 26-pin pos 24 <- GPIO14 / TXD0 J8 pin 8

g.text: GND 26-pin pos 4 shared at the star point, sheet 2

g.desc: GND 26-pin pos 4 shared at the star point, sheet 2

g.text: The single crossing in the middle of this sheet is the whole point of the drawing.

g.desc: The single crossing in the middle of this sheet is the whole point of the drawing.

g.text: Straight-through, TX to TX, is the classic error, and it is silent: two push-pull

g.desc: Straight-through, TX to TX, is the classic error, and it is silent: two push-pull

g.text: outputs facing each other, neither receiver ever seeing an edge. Nothing is damaged

g.desc: outputs facing each other, neither receiver ever seeing an edge. Nothing is damaged

g.text: and nothing works.

g.desc: and nothing works.

g.text: CHECK IT WITH A METER, POWER OFF, before trusting it: continuity from Due position 23

g.desc: CHECK IT WITH A METER, POWER OFF, before trusting it: continuity from Due position 23

g.text: to Pi pin 10, and from Due position 24 to Pi pin 8. If either reads open, or if

g.desc: to Pi pin 10, and from Due position 24 to Pi pin 8. If either reads open, or if

g.text: position 23 rings out to pin 8, the harness is wrong.

g.desc: position 23 rings out to pin 8, the harness is wrong.

g.text: THE DUE'S USART0 IS USED, NOT ITS UART. The UART on D0 / D1 is wired to the ATmega16U2

g.desc: THE DUE'S USART0 IS USED, NOT ITS UART. The UART on D0 / D1 is wired to the ATmega16U2

g.text: on the Programming port and is what bossac and the serial console use; taking it would

g.desc: on the Programming port and is what bossac and the serial console use; taking it would

g.text: cost the flashing path. USART0 appears on D18 / D19 as peripheral A on PA11 / PA10

g.desc: cost the flashing path. USART0 appears on D18 / D19 as peripheral A on PA11 / PA10

g.text: (ArduinoCore-sam variant table) and is otherwise unused on this board.

g.desc: (ArduinoCore-sam variant table) and is otherwise unused on this board.

g.text: On the Pi, GPIO14 and GPIO15 are ALT0 = TXD0 / RXD0, which is the PL011. Bare metal,

g.desc: On the Pi, GPIO14 and GPIO15 are ALT0 = TXD0 / RXD0, which is the PL011. Bare metal,

g.text: the alt-function routing is a register write, and none of the Linux-side "PL011 is

g.desc: the alt-function routing is a register write, and none of the Linux-side "PL011 is

g.text: taken by Bluetooth, use the mini-UART" advice applies - there is no Linux here.

g.desc: taken by Bluetooth, use the mini-UART" advice applies - there is no Linux here.

g.text: THE SERIES RESISTORS AND THE IDLE PULL-UPS

g.desc: THE SERIES RESISTORS AND THE IDLE PULL-UPS

g.text: R8 TO R11, 220 ohm, ONE PER PIN - four of them, not two. A resistor in the middle of a

g.desc: R8 TO R11, 220 ohm, ONE PER PIN - four of them, not two. A resistor in the middle of a

g.text: wire protects whichever end is on the far side of it from a fault injected on the near

g.desc: wire protects whichever end is on the far side of it from a fault injected on the near

g.text: side. One at each pin means every pin on this link has 220 ohm between it and anything

g.desc: side. One at each pin means every pin on this link has 220 ohm between it and anything

g.text: that goes wrong on the wire, wherever the fault comes from.

g.desc: that goes wrong on the wire, wherever the fault comes from.

g.text: fault current if 5 V lands on a line:

g.desc: fault current if 5 V lands on a line:

g.text: (5 V - 3.3 V - 0.7 V) / 220 ohm = 4.5 mA into the clamp diode

g.desc: (5 V - 3.3 V - 0.7 V) / 220 ohm = 4.5 mA into the clamp diode

g.text: Survivable, where a direct connection is not. It is a mitigation, not permission - see

g.desc: Survivable, where a direct connection is not. It is a mitigation, not permission - see

g.text: the warning opposite.

g.desc: the warning opposite.

g.text: Cost at signalling speed: against roughly 30 pF of jumper capacitance, 440 ohm

g.desc: Cost at signalling speed: against roughly 30 pF of jumper capacitance, 440 ohm

g.text: end-to-end gives a 13 ns time constant, against an 8.7 us bit at 115200 baud. Three

g.desc: end-to-end gives a 13 ns time constant, against an 8.7 us bit at 115200 baud. Three

g.text: orders of magnitude of margin.

g.desc: orders of magnitude of margin.

g.text: R12 AND R13, 10 kohm, ARE NOT OPTIONAL, and the reason is specific. An idle UART line

g.desc: R12 AND R13, 10 kohm, ARE NOT OPTIONAL, and the reason is specific. An idle UART line

g.text: is HIGH. GPIO15 - the Pi's receiver - powers up with a DEFAULT PULL-DOWN (BCM2835 ARM

g.desc: is HIGH. GPIO15 - the Pi's receiver - powers up with a DEFAULT PULL-DOWN (BCM2835 ARM

g.text: Peripherals table 6-31 lists GPIO9-27 as "Low"), so with the Due unplugged, unpowered

g.desc: Peripherals table 6-31 lists GPIO9-27 as "Low"), so with the Due unplugged, unpowered

g.text: or still in reset, the Pi's RX sits LOW. A UART receiver reads a continuously low line

g.desc: or still in reset, the Pi's RX sits LOW. A UART receiver reads a continuously low line

g.text: as a BREAK condition and a stream of framing errors - not as "quiet".

g.desc: as a BREAK condition and a stream of framing errors - not as "quiet".

g.text: R12 and R13 make the idle state HIGH in copper, at both ends, so that "the other board

g.desc: R12 and R13 make the idle state HIGH in copper, at both ends, so that "the other board

g.text: is not there" is indistinguishable from "the other board has nothing to say". Each

g.desc: is not there" is indistinguishable from "the other board has nothing to say". Each

g.text: pulls to its OWN board's 3.3 V rail. Never across the link: that would make one board

g.desc: pulls to its OWN board's 3.3 V rail. Never across the link: that would make one board

g.text: partly power the other's pin.

g.desc: partly power the other's pin.

g.text: driven-low level at the receiver, worst case:

g.desc: driven-low level at the receiver, worst case:

g.text: against VIL max of 0.9 V at the Pi and 0.99 V at the Due. Fine.

g.desc: against VIL max of 0.9 V at the Pi and 0.99 V at the Due. Fine.

g.text: GROUND, AND WHAT IS DELIBERATELY NOT IN THIS HARNESS

g.desc: GROUND, AND WHAT IS DELIBERATELY NOT IN THIS HARNESS

g.text: This harness is TWO WIRES: TX and RX. It does not carry a ground wire, and adding one

g.desc: This harness is TWO WIRES: TX and RX. It does not carry a ground wire, and adding one

g.text: is a mistake. Both boards already reach ground at the star point on PS3's negative

g.desc: is a mistake. Both boards already reach ground at the star point on PS3's negative

g.text: terminal, the only place grounds meet in this system (sheet 2). A third ground wire

g.desc: terminal, the only place grounds meet in this system (sheet 2). A third ground wire

g.text: strung directly between the two boards closes a loop with those two star legs, and the

g.desc: strung directly between the two boards closes a loop with those two star legs, and the

g.text: loop it closes encircles the 1 A load return.

g.desc: loop it closes encircles the 1 A load return.

g.text: The GND label drawn at the Due's position 4 on this sheet is that same net, reached

g.desc: The GND label drawn at the Due's position 4 on this sheet is that same net, reached

g.text: through the star. It is drawn so the return path for these signals is explicit rather

g.desc: through the star. It is drawn so the return path for these signals is explicit rather

g.text: than assumed. It is not a licence to run a jumper.

g.desc: than assumed. It is not a licence to run a jumper.

g.text: IF, AND ONLY IF, the two boards have no other common reference - testing the link on

g.desc: IF, AND ONLY IF, the two boards have no other common reference - testing the link on

g.text: its own, with no load supply present - run a single ground wire here, and know that you

g.desc: its own, with no load supply present - run a single ground wire here, and know that you

g.text: are outside the drawn topology. Take it out when the load supply goes in.

g.desc: are outside the drawn topology. Take it out when the load supply goes in.
